claim_boundary: Ticket 28 implements the proof trichotomy requested by the user: search for counterexamples, record finite evidence for no counterexample, and isolate the contrapositive bridge. It does not prove or disprove RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

attempts.attempt: Use the user's proof trichotomy explicitly: search for a finite counterexample signal, identify the theorem needed to prove no counterexample exists, and state the contrapositive bridge that would turn an off-critical zero into a bounded contradiction.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Exhibit a certified non-trivial zeta zero with real part not equal to 1/2, or an equivalent failed RH theorem.
Prove every non-trivial zero lies on the critical line with explicit control over all heights.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: Finite Mertens data cannot control the zeta zero set.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Show that any off-critical zero forces a finite Li/kernel/correlator contradiction below a computable index.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: The project still lacks a uniform tail theorem converting an off-critical zero into a bounded contradiction.

mertens_stress.stress_observable: M(n)/sqrt(n), where M is the Mertens summatory function
mertens_stress.interpretation: This is an RH-compatible finite stress observable, not an RH-equivalent proof or counterexample. A certified RH counterexample still requires an off-critical zeta zero or an equivalent failed theorem.

bounded_result.strongest_closed_statement: No RH counterexample was produced by the finite Mertens stress scan.

attempts.obstruction: The finite stress scan is compatible with RH but does not see zeta zeros. The missing object is still a tail-uniform zero or positivity theorem.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every hypothetical off-critical zero produces a computably bounded violation of a formally verified positivity kernel, with all remaining zeros controlled by an explicit tail majorant.
attempts.next_experiment: Synthesize the tail majorant as a symbolic inequality and run CEGIS against off-critical surrogate zeros.
attempts.claim_boundary: No RH proof and no certified RH counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Replace the refuted quotient-only rank with an exact affine-cylinder descent test. Each residue cylinder is followed only while its valuation word is certified by the available 2-adic bits; if the affine coefficient becomes contracting and the threshold is below the cylinder base, every lift in that cylinder descends.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Find an odd n whose accelerated orbit never reaches 1, or a non-trivial positive integer cycle.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: Finite search and false-cycle elimination are not exhaustive over all odd integers.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Prove every odd n>1 eventually descends below n, then use strong induction.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: The exact cylinder cover still has needs_split states at the tested modulus.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Assume a minimal divergent orbit or non-trivial cycle exists; force it into an exact cylinder that descends below its start.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: A minimal counterexample can hide in a cylinder whose valuation prefix needs more 2-adic information.

stopping_scan.interpretation: If every odd n>1 eventually descends below n, Collatz follows by strong induction. This scan supplies finite evidence only; it does not prove the universal descent theorem.

threshold_or_cycle_examples.interpretation: n=1 is the known fixed cycle; every larger lift in this cylinder descends.

cylinder_descent.interpretation: Exact affine cylinders prove descent for many full lift families. The remaining states are not Collatz counterexamples; they are cylinders whose valuation prefix consumes all available 2-adic bits before a strict affine contraction is certified.

bounded_result.strongest_closed_statement: For every listed all_lift_descent cylinder, the exact accelerated affine map sends every positive odd lift in that cylinder below its starting value.

attempts.obstruction: At 2^22, the exact cover still contains needs_split cylinders. They are not counterexamples, but they prevent promotion to a global strong-induction proof.
attempts.candidate_theorem: There exists a finite or recursively certified adaptive 2-adic cylinder partition such that every non-1 cylinder has an exact affine descent word; then no minimal Collatz counterexample can exist.
attempts.next_experiment: Run adaptive splitting only on needs_split cylinders and synthesize a debt-aware priority rule that proves termination of the split process.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof, no divergent orbit, and no non-trivial positive integer cycle found.

attempts.attempt: Treat Goldbach as a least-counterexample problem: scan the finite base for a direct counterexample, then isolate the exact large-even lower-bound theorem needed to prove no larger counterexample exists.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Find an even integer n>=4 with no representation n=p+q by primes p,q.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: Finite search cannot rule out a larger counterexample.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Prove a positive lower bound for the Goldbach representation count for every even n above an explicit cutoff.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: No explicit positive lower-bound theorem has been proved inside PrimeProject.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Assume the least Goldbach counterexample N exists; combine finite verification below B with a tail theorem forcing N<=B.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: The analytic cutoff has not been pushed below the verified finite base.

finite_witness_scan.interpretation: This is a witness scan for the finite base region. It is not a Goldbach proof unless paired with an explicit theorem covering every even integer above the limit.

bounded_result.strongest_closed_statement: Every even integer 4<=n<=2,000,000 has a displayed first-prime witness in the scan.

attempts.obstruction: The project still lacks an explicit large-even lower-bound theorem with all constants effective.
attempts.candidate_theorem: For every even n>N0, the Goldbach representation count is positive, and N0 is no larger than the committed finite witness scan limit.
attempts.next_experiment: Build a constant ledger for main term minus error and reject any ineffective exceptional-zero dependency.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Goldbach proof and no Goldbach counterexample found up to the finite scan limit.

attempts.attempt: Separate exact gap 2 from bounded-gap evidence. The finite scan records exact twin pairs, while the proof route demands a functional that remains positive on unbounded scales and fails under exact-gap deletion.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Certify a largest twin-prime pair and prove no larger pair exists.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: The finite scan cannot certify absence above the limit.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Prove a positive exact-gap-2 lower-bound functional on infinitely many scales.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: Bounded-gap statistics remain insufficient unless they collapse under gap-2 deletion.

trichotomy_routes.closure_condition: Assume a last twin pair exists; force contradiction by showing exact-gap-2 mass survives beyond that scale.
trichotomy_routes.open_blocker: No exact-gap-2 lower-bound functional has been proved beyond finite data.

finite_exact_gap_scan.interpretation: A finite list of twin primes is evidence only. A Twin Prime counterexample would require a certified last twin pair, and a proof requires an exact-gap-2 lower bound at arbitrarily large scales.

bounded_result.strongest_closed_statement: The scan finds exact twin-prime pairs up to 10,000,000, including the listed last pair.

attempts.obstruction: The scan does not imply infinitely many exact gap-2 pairs, and no last-pair contradiction theorem is closed.
attempts.candidate_theorem: An exact-gap-2 lower-bound functional is positive on infinitely many dyadic intervals and is destroyed by deleting every exact gap-2 pair.
attempts.next_experiment: Search sieve features whose finite value collapses under exact-gap deletion but remains stable under bounded-gap noise.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified largest twin-prime pair.
